title: The Anthropology of Engineering

{{Quote|As I sat watching the howlrunners, the walls of the safari dome seemed to melt away. For a brief moment, I was part of the pack, hunting and howling with these magnificent predators. I have spent the rest of my life trying to recapture that feeling, from the cockpit of a starfighter.|Jo Ewsli, ''The Anthropology of Engineering''|<ref name="NEGVV" />}}
'''''The Anthropology of Engineering''''' was a work [[Author/Legends|authored]] by the [[Incom Corporation/Legends|Incom Corporation]] [[Engineer/Legends|engineer]] [[Jo Ewsli]].<ref name="NEGVV" /> At some point by [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]],<ref name="Date">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of ''[[Rebel Force: Trapped]]'', which includes the appearance of an [[I-7 Howlrunner]], to [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]].</ref> Ewsli had designed the [[I-7 Howlrunner]] [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]], which was named after the [[Diet/Legends|omnivorous]] [[Howlrunner/Legends|howlrunner]] creature of<ref name="NEGVV" /> the [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]]<ref name="EAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} — Based on corresponding data for [[Kamar system/Legends|Kamar system]]</ref> [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="EGAS">[[The Essential Guide to Alien Species (real-world book)|''The Essential Guide to Alien Species'']]</ref> [[Kamar/Legends|Kamar]]. In ''The Anthropology of Engineering'', the engineer,<ref name="NEGVV" /> who at one point had visited Kamar,<ref name="EGAS" /> narrated how<ref name="NEGVV" /> he<ref name="EGAS" /> had observed a pack of [[Hunting/Legends|hunting]] howlrunners from inside a [[safari dome]]. In that passage, Ewsli also noted that he had experienced a moment where he felt like a part of the pack, and that he had subsequently strived for his entire life to design a starfighter that would help its [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] recreate the feeling.<ref name="NEGVV" />

==Behind the scenes==
''The Anthropology of Engineering'' was mentioned in the [[2003]] reference book ''[[The New Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels]]'' by [[W. Haden Blackman]].<ref name="NEGVV" />
